title: net/http Transport Ownership, Redirects, Bodies, and Timeouts · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in owned bounded net/http retrieval adapter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Configure and reuse one owned Transport with explicit proxy, dial, TLS, idle pool, compression, limits, and CloseIdleConnections policy.
Build safe retrieval requests with context, honest User-Agent and contact, Accept, validators, method, credentials, and trace identity.
Use CheckRedirect to reapply authorization, URL, credentials, method, DNS, loop, and hop budgets while retaining the chain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Transport Reuse
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 2337 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove Transport reuse, request identity, redirect checks, body limits, deadlines, and closure; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure and reuse one owned Transport with explicit proxy, dial, TLS, idle pool, compression, limits, and CloseIdleConnections policy.

Inspect the Transport Reuse trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Timeout Context
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-timeout-context-fkioxg" that uses case 3455 to prove Transport reuse, request identity, redirect checks, body limits, deadlines, and closure. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Timeout Context.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Timeout Context into observable performance evidence.
